_note: Ad-grammar families (composition archetypes). Each visual format in format-composition.json inherits one of these and overrides specifics. Adapted from tae-ad-studio's archetype methodology + product-hero + native-UI + a CONTRAST fallback for brands that ban before/after.

PRODUCT_HERO.composition: One clear focal subject — the product (or its key ingredient) — on a rule-of-thirds anchor against a clean, uncluttered background. Generous negative space on one side or third reserved for the headline. Props arranged sparingly; never crowd the product or obscure the label.
PRODUCT_HERO.style: photorealistic studio product photography, controlled soft lighting, crisp detail on the label
PRODUCT_HERO.grammarNote: The product is the subject and its label is legible. If the product isn't instantly recognizable and premium, it failed.

TESTIMONIAL_NATIVE.genre: social-proof testimonial styled like a candid user post
TESTIMONIAL_NATIVE.composition: The testifier in close-to-medium portrait, authentic not posed. Product appears incidentally (in hand or on a shelf, off-center). A large clear area (lower-left or top) is reserved for the quote/headline.
TESTIMONIAL_NATIVE.style: documentary / UGC photography, natural skin tones, slight grain, real domestic setting
TESTIMONIAL_NATIVE.grammarNote: The person's voice is the hook; the image supports them. If it looks like a studio ad, it failed.

STAT_LED_AUTHORITY.composition: Strong negative space (40-50% of canvas) on the left or top reserved for one large stat/number plus a short supporting line. Product or ingredient anchors the opposite corner. High contrast between the clean stat zone and the rich product zone.
STAT_LED_AUTHORITY.mood: confident, credible, quietly scientific — not cold
STAT_LED_AUTHORITY.style: editorial still-life, controlled lighting, precise composition, premium finish
STAT_LED_AUTHORITY.grammarNote: The number does the heavy lifting; keep its zone clean enough to render a large figure legibly at any size.

PROBLEM_AGITATION.composition: The subject embodies the problem through a recognizable gesture, expression, or environment — not the product. Close crop. Product absent or tiny. A large text zone across the top or bottom third for the hook.
PROBLEM_AGITATION.mood: subdued, honest, recognizable — never depressing or exaggerated
PROBLEM_AGITATION.style: documentary close-up, natural imperfect light, slightly desaturated, grain
PROBLEM_AGITATION.grammarNote: The scroller must recognize themselves within one second. Specificity beats beauty. Respect brand rules — no distress or shame where the brand forbids it.

LIFESTYLE_ASPIRATION.composition: A real person mid-moment doing the thing the product enables (not holding it). Product is secondary — off-center or in soft focus. Large negative space in the lower-left or lower-third for copy. The setting communicates the desired life.
LIFESTYLE_ASPIRATION.style: lifestyle editorial photography, soft natural light, slight film look, shallow depth of field, warm grade
LIFESTYLE_ASPIRATION.grammarNote: Sell the life, not the bottle. If the product were removed, the image should still communicate the promise.

EDUCATIONAL_DEMYSTIFY.composition: Diagrammatic or deconstructed layout — ingredient(s), product component(s), or a simple conceptual visual (cross-section, arrow, comparison, numbered steps) on a clean surface. Large headline zone top or left. Lots of negative space.
EDUCATIONAL_DEMYSTIFY.mood: curious, clear, instructional without being dry, a little playful
EDUCATIONAL_DEMYSTIFY.style: clean studio photography or tasteful minimal illustration, high clarity, controlled shadows
EDUCATIONAL_DEMYSTIFY.grammarNote: The image must teach something at a glance. If the viewer cannot point to what they learned, it did not earn its place in the feed.

BEFORE_AFTER.composition: Split the canvas (50/50 or 60/40 hero-right). Left half shows the before state; right half shows the after state — same subject, same framing. Lower third reserved for headline + CTA. Keep the contrast legible at thumbnail size.
BEFORE_AFTER.mood: honest, quiet confidence — no miracle-cure drama
BEFORE_AFTER.style: clean editorial photography, diffuse soft light, minimal-retouch feel, shallow depth of field on the after side
BEFORE_AFTER.grammarNote: The eye tracks left-to-right and lands on the outcome. Use only where the brand permits before/after; never doctor the result.

CONTRAST.composition: Two states or options side by side — old way vs new way, this vs that — with no editorializing arrows. Balanced split; the visual makes the winner obvious. Product anchors the winning side. Lower third reserved for the headline.
CONTRAST.style: clean editorial photography, even soft lighting on both sides
CONTRAST.grammarNote: The visual makes the winner obvious without claiming a medical transformation of a person's body.

NATIVE_UI.composition: Mimic a real interface (Notes app, iMessage/text thread, comment section, ChatGPT, review card) with believable, pixel-accurate UI chrome. The message/text is the focus. Minimal or no product; if present, a small inline thumbnail.
NATIVE_UI.mood: unfiltered, personal — like something you weren't meant to see
NATIVE_UI.style: pixel-accurate UI mimicry, flat, screenshot realism
NATIVE_UI.grammarNote: It must read as a real screenshot, not a designed ad. If it looks polished, it failed.
